retrieved_docs.title: Drug and Alcohol Use (Archived) -- Etiology -- Genetic Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Among type 1 and type 2 alcoholics (typologies developed by Cloninger), type 1 alcoholics are characterized by the late onset of problem drinking, the rapid development of behavioral tolerance to alcohol, prominent guilt and anxiety related to drinking, and infrequent fighting and arrests when drinking. Cloninger also termed this subtype “milieu-limited,” which emphasizes the etiologic role of environmental factors. In contrast, type 2 alcoholics are characterized by early onset of an inability to abstain from alcohol, frequent fighting and arrests when drinking, and the absence of guilt and fear concerning drinking. Cloninger postulated that transmission of alcoholism in type 2 alcoholics was from fathers to sons, hence term the male-limited alcoholism. Type 1 alcoholics are characterized by high-reward dependence, high-harm avoidance, and low-novelty seeking. In contrast, type 2 alcoholics are characterized by high-novelty seeking, low-harm avoidance, and low-reward dependence. Cloninger also hypothesized that specific neurotransmitter systems underlie personality structure. Specifically, dopamine is hypothesized to modulate novelty seeking, characterized by frequent exploratory behavior and intensely pleasurable responses to novel stimuli. Serotonin is hypothesized to modulate harm avoidance, which is a tendency to respond intensely to aversive stimuli and their conditioned signals. Finally, norepinephrine is hypothesized to modulate reward dependence or the resistance to extinction of previously rewarded behavior.

retrieved_docs.title: Drug and Alcohol Use (Archived) -- Etiology -- Environmental Factors
retrieved_docs.content: It has been estimated that there is a 7-fold risk of alcoholism in first-degree relatives of alcohol-dependent individuals, with male relatives of male alcohol-dependent individuals having the greatest risk for the disorder.

retrieved_docs.title: Fetal Alcohol Syndrome -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: The history that is associated with undiagnosed fetal alcohol spectrum disorders is fairly wide. In neonates, it is crucial to get a good prenatal history to determine prenatal alcohol exposure. For older children and young adults, the primary indicative history will be those areas pertaining to neurocognitive and behavioral impairment. Their history will point to the fact that those with fetal alcohol spectrum disorders have a high incidence of emotional and behavioral problems. Past experience with the juvenile justice system or foster care system, having a sibling with fetal alcohol spectrum disorders, recurrent unemployment, a history of substance abuse, and a history of inappropriate sexual behaviors such as improper touching and inappropriate exposure are some of the historical findings that should raise the index of suspicion for fetal alcohol spectrum disorders.

retrieved_docs.content: Dig deep into a history of Disrupted familial and social relationships, failure to fulfill responsibilities at school or work (e.g., loss of job, poor grades in school. Ask questions about any past history of violent behavior, child abuse, financial problems, and sexual history (multiple partners, sex habits, etc.)

retrieved_docs.title: Conduct Disorder -- Etiology -- Parental and Family
retrieved_docs.content: A home environment that lacks structure and adequate supervision with frequent marital conflicts between parents, inconsistent discipline leads to maladaptive behavior. Harsh parenting with verbal and physical aggression towards children Children exposed to frequent domestic violence The family history of criminality and disruptive behaviors in caregivers. Substance abuse, particularly alcohol dependence in parents Living in low social, economic conditions with overcrowding and unemployment leads to economic and social stress with lack of adequate parenting.

retrieved_docs.title: Drug and Alcohol Use (Archived) -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: As with most behavioral and psychiatric disorders, the interplay between genetic risk, temperamental traits, and the environment may predispose to early use of substances of abuse. Once exposed to substances, brain reward systems reinforce substance use, resulting in repeated use and lower ability to control substance use.

retrieved_docs.title: Drug and Alcohol Use (Archived) -- History and Physical -- History
retrieved_docs.content: A detailed history is necessary to establish the diagnosis of drug abuse. A patient’s social history can provide information about risk factors for unhealthy substance use and the impact of substance use on the patient’s role functioning. Look for a partner with a substance use disorder, poor socio-economic society, violence, high school alcohol, and other drug availability. A family history of substance use disorder (SUD) has been shown to be a risk factor for developing a substance abuse disorder. Heritability estimates range from: 50% to 70% for alcohol use disorder From 34% to 78% for cannabis use disorder 42% to 79% percent for cocaine use disorder 23% to 54% for opioid use disorder

retrieved_docs.content: Is there a family history of drug use ? If so, have there been any fatalities from substance use? It is important to be aware of any substance use by other family members because family influences the individual’s risk of substance use.

retrieved_docs.title: Florida: Domestic Violence -- Etiology -- Risk Factors for Domestic and Family Violence
retrieved_docs.content: Aggressive behavior as a youth Antisocial personality disorder Individuals with disabilities Corporal punishment within the household Pregnancy Economic stress Disparities in educational or occupational levels between partners, particularly when women have higher academic or occupational attainment than their spouses Low self-esteem Family history of violence Limited education Inadequate parenting History of psychiatric issues Marital discord Infidelity Multiple children Poor legal sanctions or enforcement of laws History of childhood abuse Unemployment Substance use disorder, including the use of alcohol and drugs, which is strongly associated with a high probability of violence. Alcohol use particularly predicts acute injury, with approximately half of domestic violence incidents involving an intoxicated partner at the time of the assault. Intimate partner violence linked to new cases of HIV.

retrieved_docs.content: (disorders caused by alcohol) is a subtype of اضطراب تعاطي المواد الإدمانية, illustrating the hierarchical relationship between alcohol‑specific disorders and broader addictive‑behavior diagnoses. Disorders typically diagnosed in infancy are related to ذُهان الطفولة غير المحدد، حالة راهنة أو نشطة, indicating that active unspecified childhood psychosis falls within the early‑life diagnostic spectrum. Collectively, these hierarchical classifications and shared symptomatology provide a comprehensive framework for assessing, diagnosing, and delivering targeted care across organic psychosis, depressive disorders, behavioral dysregulation, encephalitic conditions, and substance‑use disorders, while highlighting the clinical relevance of attention deficits, daytime sleepiness, and the overlap between organic psychosis and schizophrenic mania.

retrieved_docs.content: Drug Use and Addiction is related to اضطراب تعاطي الكحول, indicating that general patterns of substance use and dependence intersect with the specific disorder of alcohol misuse, underscoring the clinical importance of evaluating and treating both broader addictive behaviors and alcohol‑specific pathology in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 2 -- Community 367
retrieved_docs.content: Alcohol and drug rehabilitation/detoxification is linked to متلازمة الامتناع عن تعاطي الكحول (alcohol abstinence syndrome), which in turn includes الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) as a component of its clinical picture. الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) comprises a broad spectrum of psychotic and affective conditions. It contains ذُهان الطفولة غير المحدد، حالة راهنة أو نشطة (unspecified childhood schizophrenia, active or current state), reflecting that disorders usually diagnosed in infancy have a broader relationship with early‑onset psychotic presentations. Within the SMQ group are several depressive entities: اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة راجعة (major depressive disorder, recurrent episode), اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة واحدة (major depressive disorder, single episode), اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة واحدة في هدأة تامة (major depressive disorder, single episode in full remission), اكتئاب أَوبِيّ (obligatory depression), and التقلقل الوجداني (mood lability). Additionally, الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) is associated with عاطفة متقلصة (blunted affect), indicating affective flattening as a characteristic feature of this syndrome cluster. Together, these relationships illustrate how substance‑use treatment, early‑life psychotic disorders, various depressive states, mood instability, and blunted affect are interconnected within the framework of the SMQ and alcohol abstinence syndrome, highlighting important considerations for comprehensive patient assessment and care.

Infancy‑onset disorders are related to unspecified childhood psychosis (ذُهان الطفولة غير المحدد، حالة راهنة أو نشطة), itself a member of psychosis and psychotic disorders. Alcohol‑related disorders are classified as an inverse subclass of substance‑use disorder (اضطراب تعاطي المواد الإدمانية). Both recurrent and single‑episode major depressive episodes are also placed within the psychosis and psychotic disorders grouping, highlighting the potential overlap of depressive and psychotic features and the intricate interplay between organic, psychotic, and mood‑related conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2620
retrieved_docs.content: Alcoholic hepatitis (تَشَمَّعٌ كُحولِيّ) is a liver disorder caused by excessive alcohol consumption that can progress to hepatic cirrhosis (تَشَمُّع كَبِدي). This relationship highlights the potential evolution from an inflammatory, potentially reversible condition to an irreversible stage of extensive liver scarring, emphasizing the importance of early detection and treatment of alcoholic hepatitis to prevent the development of cirrhosis.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2591
retrieved_docs.content: The condition إصابة الرأس المغلقة (closed head injury) is clinically associated with تعاطي الكحول (alcohol consumption); alcohol use increases the likelihood of sustaining a closed head injury, and patients presenting with such injuries often have a history of recent alcohol intake. Recognizing this association is important for clinicians when assessing trauma patients, guiding both acute management and preventive counseling regarding alcohol use.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 4 -- Community 2868
retrieved_docs.content: Abnormal chest X‑ray findings (الأشعة السينية على الصَدْر غير طبيعية) are clinically associated with alcohol consumption (تعاطي الكحول). In patients who consume alcohol, clinicians should consider that pulmonary imaging may reveal atypical or pathological changes, which can stem from alcohol‑related lung injury, aspiration events, infections, or other respiratory complications. Recognizing this association prompts healthcare providers to inquire about alcohol use when interpreting abnormal chest radiographs, to assess for potential alcohol‑related pulmonary disease, and to incorporate appropriate counseling or treatment for alcohol misuse into the overall patient care plan.

Childhood‑onset disorders are related to unspecified active childhood psychosis, a member of the psychosis and psychotic disorders (SMQ) group. Depression excluding suicide and self‑harm (الاكتئاب (باستثناء الانتحار وإيذاء النفس) (SMQ)) aggregates a broad spectrum of depressive subtypes—including recurrent and single‑episode major depressive disorder (in full or complete remission), reactive, endogenous, melancholic, atypical, and obsessive‑type depression—as well as substance‑use disorder, reflecting frequent comorbidity with addictive behaviors. This SMQ depression construct also includes transient and continuous disorders of awakening onset/maintenance and habituation and is classified within post‑natal depression (excluding psychosis).
